serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Korean Cheesy Tteokbokki Stew Noodles is approximately 450 g, comprising a generous bowl of spicy stew broth with noodles, rice cakes, cheese, corn, and hotdog.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central component foods: wheat noodles (cooked), tteok rice cakes, gochujang, processed cheese, corn (canned), pork hotdog sausage, and spicy broth; weighted composite per typical Korean tteokbokki stew noodle recipe proportions
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Korean Cheesy Tteokbokki Stew Noodles exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component foods using a weighted composite approach based on typical recipe proportions: wheat noodles (cooked) ~35%, tteok rice cakes ~25%, spicy broth ~15%, processed cheese ~8%, hotdog sausage ~8%, corn ~5%, gochujang ~4%. Values represent per-100g of the assembled dish.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of rice cakes and wheat noodles; analogous estimate for tteokbokki stew noodle composite. Reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Rice cakes GI ~82; cooked wheat noodles GI ~47; composite weighted estimate ~58.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Korean Cheesy Tteokbokki Stew Noodles. GI estimated as a weighted composite: tteok rice cakes (GI ~82, University of Sydney GI Database) contribute ~25% of carbohydrate mass; cooked wheat noodles (GI ~47, University of Sydney GI Database) contribute ~55% of carbohydrate mass; remaining carbohydrate from corn, gochujang, and broth (GI ~55 average) contribute ~20%. Weighted GI = (0.25 × 82) + (0.55 × 47) + (0.20 × 55) = 20.5 + 25.85 + 11.0 = 57.35, rounded to 58. GL per serving = (58 × 21.5 × 4.5) / 100 = 56.1, rounded to 56.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~2340 mg per 450 g serving) from spicy stew broth, gochujang, processed cheese, and hotdog sausage
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium content per serving, which can exceed the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg sodium in a single meal; if consumed, portion size should be strictly reduced and high-sodium condiments avoided elsewhere in the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium-to-high glycemic index (~58) driven by tteok rice cakes (GI ~82) and high carbohydrate load (~96.75 g carbohydrate per 450 g serving), resulting in a high glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this dish due to its high glycemic load per serving driven by tteok rice cakes and wheat noodles; portion reduction, pairing with non-starchy vegetables, and monitoring postprandial blood glucose are strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (~2340 mg per serving), saturated fat from processed cheese and hotdog sausage (~8.55 g per serving), and trans fat from processed ingredients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dish due to its combination of high sodium, saturated fat from processed cheese and hotdog sausage, and potential trans fat content, all of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk; occasional consumption in small portions is preferable to regular intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Korean Cheesy Tteokbokki Stew Noodles (치즈 떡볶이 스튜 국수) is a Korean-inspired comfort food dish consisting of chewy tteok (Korean rice cakes), wheat noodles, corn, processed cheese, and hotdog sausage served in a spicy gochujang-based stew broth. The dish is a fusion of the classic Korean street food tteokbokki with the addition of noodles, cheese, and hotdog, creating a rich, spicy, and creamy flavour profile. The tteok provides a dense, chewy texture while the noodles add a softer bite; the melted cheese tempers the heat of the gochujang broth and adds creaminess. This dish is popular in Korean casual dining and has been adapted for the Singapore market by restaurants such as The Noodle Place. Nutritionally, it is a high-carbohydrate, moderate-protein, moderate-fat dish with a medium glycemic index (~58) but a high glycemic load per standard serving (~56.5) due to the large portion of rice cakes and noodles. Sodium content is very high (~520 mg per 100 g), making it a concern for individuals with hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The dish contains dairy (cheese), gluten (wheat noodles), and soy (gochujang, hotdog), and is not suitable for vegetarians due to the hotdog sausage. It is best consumed occasionally and in controlled portions by individuals managing blood glucose, blood pressure, or cardiovascular risk.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Korean Cheesy Tteokbokki Stew Noodles exists in any approved primary regional source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component foods using a weighted composite approach. GI is estimated from analogous foods (tteok rice cakes and wheat noodles) using the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation methodology and absence of a direct source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central component foods; no direct approved-source entry exists for this specific dish. Values should be treated as indicative and updated if a direct HPB or equivalent regional source entry becomes available. 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reliably reported for the component foods in USDA FoodData Central at the level of detail required for this composite. 3. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not consistently reported across all component foods in the composite estimation. 4. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported with sufficient consistency across the component foods used in the composite estimation. 5. GI is an analogous estimate based on weighted carbohydrate contributions from tteok rice cakes (GI ~82) and wheat noodles (GI ~47) per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). 6. Halal status is uncertain as hotdog sausage sourced from The Noodle Place - Circular Road may or may not be halal-certified; is_halal is set to false pending confirmation. 7. The food_name has been normalised from the input emoji-prefixed menu name to the canonical English form 'Korean Cheesy Tteokbokki Stew Noodles'; 'toppoki' is a common romanisation variant of 'tteokbokki' and is preserved in aliases.
